title: Occult Contacts
description: Recurring contacts in the occult underground — patrons, suppliers, rivals, and frenemies that a crew might cultivate or inherit.

entries.result: The Pharmacist. Supplies compounds, preparations, and materials that the regulated market doesn't carry. No questions. No credit. Perfect discretion. Occasional curiosity about what the materials are for.
The Cataloguer. Knows the provenance of almost any significant occult object that has changed hands in the last forty years. Will share for a price, usually information rather than coin.
The Warden. Manages a private detention situation that nobody with official standing will acknowledge. Rents access for short terms to parties with appropriate references.
The Archivist. Maintains a collection of primary documents on defunct organizations, dead practitioners, and failed workings. Extremely valuable. Extremely paranoid.
The Intermediary. Carries messages between parties who can't contact each other directly. Takes no position on content. Has heard everything. Has said nothing.
The Printer. Produces documentation — licenses, identities, records, letters of introduction — at a quality that passes examination. Slow, expensive, and worth it.
The Safekeeper. Holds things that need holding — objects, people, information — without opening them, reading them, or asking about them. The service is real. The price is ongoing.
The Academic. Has legitimate institutional access that the crew lacks and a private research agenda that occasionally requires assistance the institution won't provide.
The Embalmer. Manages the physical aftermaths of operations involving the dead, both human and otherwise. Professional, discreet, slightly strange in ways that are not entirely explicable.

The Patron. Provides resources, access, and legitimacy in exchange for occasional unavoidable service. The relationship is good as long as what they ask doesn't require crossing a line the crew has drawn.
The Surveyor. Has mapped every significant occult site in the city, including those that officially don't exist. Will share maps for equivalents received.

The Widow. Was once the partner of a significant practitioner. Inherited the practice, the debts, the enemies, and the contacts. Has been navigating this for twenty years with no assistance from anyone.
The Liaison. Works within a legitimate institution and provides the crew advance notice of investigations, raids, and official interest in their area of operations.
The Collector. Buys significant occult items at fair market value, no questions. Has a waiting list of buyers. Has a correspondingly extensive set of enemies.
The Specialist. The only person in the city who can do a very specific thing that the crew occasionally needs done. Aware of their value. Prices accordingly.
The Courier. Moves things across the city — and occasionally further — using routes that aren't observed and contacts that aren't recorded. Absolute reliability within the terms of a contract.
The Judge. Mediates disputes between parties in the occult underground. Their decisions are binding by custom rather than law. Enforced by reputation rather than force.
The Custodian. Maintains several properties that operate as neutral ground for meetings between hostile parties. Has a good relationship with everyone because they work very hard at it.
The Apprentice. Very young. Very capable. Currently attached to a practitioner whose ethics they're beginning to question. Will be available on the market soon if they don't die first.
The Ghost. Hasn't been seen in person in years. Communicates exclusively through intermediaries and drop-boxes. Their information is always current. Their location is always wrong.
